items.title: MiMo-V2.5-Pro (Xiaomi)
items.innovation: 1.02T MoE with SWA+Global hybrid attention (6:1), strong agentic coding
items.analysis: Innovation: a 1.02T / 42B-active MoE on a hybrid-attention backbone that interleaves sliding-window attention (128-token window) with global attention at a 6:1 ratio, cutting KV-cache ~7x on long context while holding quality; paired with the MiMo Code agentic harness. Performance: vendor-reported #1 open-source on GDPVal-AA / ClawEval, and MiMo Code + V2.5-Pro beats Claude Code + Sonnet 4.6 on SWE-bench Verified (82 vs 79), SWE-bench Pro (62 vs 55), Terminal-Bench 2 (73 vs 69) — provisional and harness-coupled. Fit for RL-on-NPU: SWA-heavy hybrid attention is decode-cheap and the ~7x KV cut directly eases rollout memory on memory-bound NPUs; MIT license makes it a viable open RL base — but it is not yet on vLLM-Ascend.
items.summary: Released Apr 28 2026 (MIT open). MiMo-V2.5-Pro: 1.02T total / 42B active MoE, 1M context, hybrid attention interleaving a 128-token sliding-window attention with global attention at 6:1 (~7x KV-cache reduction). Standard MiMo-V2.5: 310B / 15B active, ~48T-token pretrain. Strong agentic coding via the MiMo Code harness (vendor benchmarks beat Claude Code + Sonnet 4.6). All numbers vendor/media — provisional.
items.ascendNote: Not yet listed on vLLM-Ascend; no Ascend port reported.

items.title: HISA: Hierarchical Indexing for Sparse Attention
items.innovation: Efficient hierarchical indexing for fine-grained sparse attention
items.analysis: Innovation: hierarchical indexing that makes fine-grained sparse attention's token/block selection cheap to compute. Fit for RL-on-NPU: the index step is the hidden cost in DSA/MSA-style sparsity; cheaper indexing helps these run efficiently — including on NPUs where custom index kernels are scarce.
items.summary: Hierarchical indexing to accelerate the token/block-selection step of fine-grained sparse attention — addressing the indexing overhead shared by DSA/MSA-style schemes.

items.title: DeepSeek-V4 (V4-Pro / V4-Flash)
items.innovation: 1.6T-param MoE with hybrid sparse attention and 1M context
items.analysis: Innovation: keeps the proven DeepSeekMoE + Multi-Token-Prediction backbone but adds a Hybrid Attention Architecture (Compressed Sparse Attention + Heavily Compressed Attention) to make 1M-token context affordable, trained on 32T+ tokens with multi-teacher distillation. Performance: positioned at the open-weight frontier (V4-Pro ~1.6T/49B active, V4-Flash 284B/13B) with strong long-context/reasoning numbers in the tech report — treat exact deltas as vendor-reported until third-party replications land. Fit for RL-on-NPU: already supported on 910B via vLLM-Ascend (custom ops + MTP KV-cache sharding for speculative decoding), so it's a realistic rollout/eval target, and its sparse attention lowers rollout memory pressure — useful given the missing vLLM sleep-mode on Ascend.
items.summary: Released Apr 2026. V4-Pro (~1.6T total / ~49B active) and V4-Flash (284B / ~13B active), both 1M-token context. Keeps DeepSeekMoE + Multi-Token Prediction; adds a Hybrid Attention Architecture (Compressed Sparse Attention + Heavily Compressed Attention) and a 32T+-token, multi-teacher-distillation data pipeline. Official technical report (PDF) on Hugging Face.
items.ascendNote: Supported on Ascend 910B via vLLM-Ascend — custom operators, KV-pool adaptation, and MTP-layer KV-cache sharding for speculative decoding.

items.title: GLM-5.2
items.innovation: Open-weight (MIT) frontier model, 1M context, coding-first
items.analysis: Innovation: an MIT-licensed, fully open-weight frontier model with a 1M-token window, tuned coding-first and shipped on a fast cadence (GLM-5 → 5.2 in months) as a deliberate open answer to export controls. Performance: reported competitive with closed models on multi-step reasoning and code generation — numbers are largely vendor/media-sourced pending independent evals. Fit for RL-on-NPU: GLM-5 is already supported on vLLM-Ascend (W8A8C8 quant), so 5.2 is likely a near-term target; its permissive license makes it an attractive base model for open RL post-training experiments on Ascend.
items.summary: Launched Jun 13 2026; MIT open weights on Hugging Face (zai-org/GLM-5.2). ~753B MoE / ~40B active, DSA-style sparse attention with IndexShare (the sparse-attention indexer is reused across each group of 4 layers instead of recomputed per layer), usable 1M context (5× over 5.1's ~200K), 131K output, 28.5T-token pretrain, dual High/Max reasoning. Media/vendor benchmarks: SWE-bench Pro 62.1 (> GPT-5.5 ~58.6), FrontierSWE 74.4, Terminal-Bench 2.1 81.0, AIME 2026 99.2, GPQA 91.2; weak on agentic Tool-Decathlon. API $1.40/$4.40 per 1M (~1/6 of GPT-5.5). Note: SWE-bench Verified/LiveCodeBench/Aider not published. All provisional. Full writeup in Blog → Dispatch 06.
items.ascendNote: GLM-5 is supported on vLLM-Ascend (perf optimizations + W8A8C8 quant); GLM-5.2 not yet listed by name.

items.title: MiniMax-M3 + MSA (MiniMax Sparse Attention)
items.innovation: New sparse attention for 1M context + native multimodal/agentic
items.analysis: Innovation: MSA (MiniMax Sparse Attention) — a blockwise sparse attention on GQA where a lightweight index branch scores KV blocks and selects a per-group Top-k, so exact attention runs over only the selected blocks. Performance: on a 109B multimodal model it matches dense GQA quality while cutting per-token attention compute ~28.4x at 1M context (~14.2x prefill / ~7.6x decode speedup on H800, per the paper). Fit for RL-on-NPU: the large decode-side speedup is exactly what helps RL rollouts on memory-bound NPUs; MiniMax-family quant (W8A8/QuaRot) exists on vLLM-Ascend though M3 isn't listed by name yet — a strong candidate once support lands.
items.summary: Released Jun 2026. MSA (MiniMax Sparse Attention) is a blockwise sparse attention on GQA: a lightweight index branch scores KV blocks and picks a per-group Top-k, then exact block-sparse attention runs over only those blocks. On a 109B multimodal model it matches GQA while cutting per-token attention compute ~28.4x at 1M context (~14.2x prefill / ~7.6x decode speedup on H800). Open-weight, native multimodal, strong agentic coding. (Shipped MiniMax-M3 is reportedly ~428B total / ~23B active; the MSA paper's experiments were on a 109B model.)
items.ascendNote: MiniMax family has W8A8/QuaRot on vLLM-Ascend (M2.x); M3 not yet listed by name.

items.title: TTKV: Temporal-Tiered KV Cache
items.innovation: Tiered KV cache across HBM/DRAM with mixed precision
items.summary: Partitions the KV cache into temporal tiers with heterogeneous capacity and precision (recent KV in faster/higher-precision tiers), using block-wise streaming attention to serve long contexts under tight memory — a practical long-context inference recipe.

items.title: RedKnot: Head-Aware KV Reuse
items.innovation: Position-independent KV reuse via per-head decomposition
items.summary: Decomposes the KV cache along KV heads to enable position-independent reuse, prefix compression, and hot/cold separation (SegPagedAttention) — improving long-context serving efficiency without retraining.

items.title: DeepSeek-V3.2 + DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA)
items.innovation: Trainable sparse attention for cheaper long-context training/inference
items.summary: DeepSeek-V3.2 introduces DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA), a learned token-selection sparse-attention scheme that cuts long-context compute while preserving quality, pushing the open-weight frontier in late 2025 / early 2026.
items.ascendNote: DeepSeek V3.x family supported on vLLM-Ascend (910B).

items.title: DeepSeekMoE
items.innovation: Fine-grained expert segmentation + isolated shared experts
items.summary: Splits FFN experts into more, smaller units at fixed param count and dedicates a few always-active shared experts to absorb common knowledge so routed experts specialize. The MoE foundation for the entire DeepSeek-V2/V3 line.

items.title: Auxiliary-Loss-Free Load Balancing
items.innovation: Per-expert routing bias nudged by load — no auxiliary loss
items.summary: Instead of penalizing imbalance with an auxiliary loss (which hurts quality), a per-expert bias is added to affinity scores before top-K and adjusted by recent load. Yields better balance and better quality; used to train DeepSeek-V3.

items.title: Mixtral of Experts
items.innovation: Open-weight sparse MoE — 8 experts/layer, top-2 routing
items.summary: Mixtral 8x7B has 47B total params but activates ~13B/token, beating Llama-2 70B on math, code, and multilingual. Apache-2.0; the most influential early open MoE release.

items.title: Qwen3 (MoE design)
items.innovation: Drops shared experts; global-batch load-balancing loss
items.summary: Family from 0.6B to 235B-total/22B-active, Apache-2.0, 119 languages. Qwen3-MoE removes the shared experts of Qwen2.5-MoE and relies on a global-batch load-balancing loss to push specialization.

items.title: Drop-Upcycling
items.innovation: Dense→MoE upcycling with partial expert re-initialization
items.summary: Pure upcycling yields near-identical, slow-to-specialize experts; Drop-Upcycling re-initializes a fraction of weights to break symmetry. A 5.9B-active MoE matches a 13B dense model at ~1/4 the training FLOPs.

items.title: Scaling Laws for Optimal MoE Sparsity
items.innovation: Optimal active-vs-total params under fixed compute
items.summary: Characterizes the total-vs-active parameter tradeoff given a compute budget, showing higher sparsity is often optimal as compute grows. Practical guidance for choosing expert counts and activation ratios.

items.title: Multi-head Latent Attention (DeepSeek-V2)
items.innovation: Low-rank joint KV compression — ~93% smaller KV cache
items.summary: MLA projects keys/values into a compact latent space so only a small latent KV vector is cached during decoding, slashing memory-bandwidth demands. Paired with DeepSeekMoE in a 236B/21B-active model; carried into V3/R1.
items.analysis: Innovation: compresses keys/values into a low-rank latent shared across heads, shrinking the KV cache ~93% while preserving quality, with decoupled RoPE to stay rotary-compatible. Performance: enables much longer context and cheaper inference at frontier scale (DeepSeek-V2/V3). Fit for RL-on-NPU: a smaller KV cache directly cuts rollout memory — meaningful on 64GB 910B where the missing sleep-mode already squeezes generation memory.

items.title: Grouped-Query Attention (GQA)
items.innovation: Query heads share KV projections in groups
items.summary: Interpolates between MHA and MQA; MHA checkpoints can be uptrained into GQA with ~5% of pretraining compute. Near-MHA quality at near-MQA speed — now the default KV-saving scheme in Llama, Mistral, and most open LLMs.

items.title: Sliding Window Attention (Mistral 7B)
items.innovation: Local window per token; stacking extends receptive field
items.summary: SWA bounds attention cost and caps the KV cache to W tokens via a rotating buffer, while layer stacking propagates info beyond a window. Popularized the SWA+GQA combo carried into 2024-2026 models.

items.title: FlashAttention-3
items.innovation: Hopper async (warp-specialization, TMA) + FP8 blocks
items.summary: Overlaps compute and data movement with warp-specialized async pipelines and interleaves block matmul with softmax, plus FP8 support. Reaches ~840 TFLOPs/s (~85% util) BF16 on H100 — 1.5–2.0x over FA-2.

items.title: Mamba-2 / SSM-Attention Duality
items.innovation: SSD framework unifying SSMs and attention; 2–8x faster
items.summary: Proves a class of SSMs is equivalent to causal linear attention via structured semiseparable matrices. Mamba-2's SSD layer expands state ~8x and trains 2–8x faster while staying competitive with Transformers.

items.title: Jamba (Transformer-Mamba hybrid)
items.innovation: Interleaved Mamba + attention (1:7) with periodic MoE
items.summary: Combines Transformer expressiveness with SSM efficiency to fit a 12B-active/52B-total model on one 80GB GPU at 256K context. Apache-2.0; scaled in Jamba-1.5.

items.title: Native Sparse Attention (NSA)
items.org: DeepSeek-AI / Peking U. / UW (ACL 2025 best paper)
items.innovation: Trainable hierarchical sparse attention, hardware-aligned
items.summary: Three parallel branches (compressed, selected blocks, local sliding) with GPU-optimized kernels so sparsity is learned during pretraining, not bolted on. Matches/beats full attention with large speedups at 64K length.
items.analysis: Innovation: a trainable, hardware-aligned hierarchical sparse attention (compressed + selected + sliding branches) where the sparsity is learned end-to-end rather than bolted on. Performance: matches full-attention quality with large long-context speedups, trained sparse from the start. Fit for RL-on-NPU: cheaper long-context attention lowers rollout cost on memory-bound NPUs, and the same DeepSeek lineage (DSA in V3.2) is what 910B inference paths are adapting.

items.title: MoBA (Mixture of Block Attention)
items.innovation: MoE-style routing applied to attention KV blocks
items.summary: Partitions context into blocks and uses a learned router to pick the most relevant blocks per query, avoiding hand-designed sparsity. Switches seamlessly between full and sparse attention; in production for Kimi long-context.

items.title: Position Interpolation (PI)
items.innovation: Linearly down-scale RoPE indices instead of extrapolating
items.summary: Rescales position indices so out-of-range positions map back inside the trained range, reaching 32K with ~1000 fine-tuning steps. The basis for NTK-aware scaling and YaRN.

items.title: YaRN
items.innovation: NTK-by-parts frequency interpolation + attention temp scaling
items.summary: Interpolates RoPE frequencies non-uniformly and adds length-dependent attention scaling, extending to 64K–128K with 10x fewer tokens than PI. The de facto RoPE-extension recipe used by Qwen and DeepSeek long-context variants.

items.title: Ring Attention
items.innovation: Distribute sequence across devices; overlap KV comm with compute
items.summary: Devices form a ring passing KV blocks while each computes blockwise attention/FFN, hiding communication behind computation — exact, no approximation. Removes per-device memory as the context bottleneck for 100M+ tokens.

items.title: LongRoPE
items.innovation: Evolutionary search over non-uniform RoPE rescaling to 2M
items.summary: Exploits two forms of positional-interpolation non-uniformity found via search, then progressively extends and readjusts at 8K to preserve short-context quality. Demonstrated 2M-token context; integrated into Phi-3.

items.title: Gemini 1.5 (1M+ context)
items.innovation: Sparse-MoE multimodal with near-perfect multi-M-token recall
items.summary: Processes up to 10M tokens with ~99.7% needle-in-haystack recall at 1M across text/video/audio. Productionized the 1M+ frontier while matching Gemini 1.0 Ultra on standard benchmarks.

items.title: ChunkKV
items.innovation: KV-cache compression at semantic-chunk granularity
items.summary: Retains contiguous semantically meaningful chunks (not per-token) and reuses indices across layers (~26.5% throughput gain). Targets long-context inference where the KV cache can consume up to 70% of GPU memory.

items.title: DeepSeek-V3 FP8 Training
items.innovation: First validated FP8 training at frontier (671B MoE) scale
items.summary: Runs most GEMMs in FP8 with 1×128 tile-wise and 128×128 block-wise scaling while keeping sensitive modules higher-precision, holding relative error under ~0.25%. Proved FP8 viable for frontier-scale training.
items.analysis: Innovation: the first validated FP8 training at frontier (671B-MoE) scale — fine-grained per-tile/per-block scaling, selective high-precision accumulation, and careful outlier handling. Performance: matched BF16 quality at large compute/memory savings, proving FP8 pretraining is production-viable. Fit for RL-on-NPU: directly motivates FP8 RL on Ascend now that the 950 series adds native FP8 — while raising the precision-alignment stakes that align-probe is built to measure.

items.title: FP4 Training Framework
items.innovation: End-to-end FP4 via differentiable estimator + outlier clamping
items.summary: Tackles FP4's severe error with a differentiable quantization estimator and outlier clamping to prevent activation collapse. Matches BF16/FP8 accuracy up to 13B params / 100B tokens.

items.title: NVFP4 Pretraining
items.innovation: Large-scale microscaled-FP4 pretraining on Blackwell
items.summary: Pretrains a 12B hybrid Mamba-Transformer on 10T tokens entirely in NVFP4 with Random Hadamard transforms, 2D consistent quantization, and stochastic rounding, matching FP8. 2–3x throughput, ~half memory.

items.title: AWQ (Activation-aware Weight Quantization)
items.innovation: Protect ~1% salient channels via activation-based scaling
items.summary: Weight-only low-bit PTQ that scales salient channels using activation statistics, preserving accuracy without reconstruction or backprop. The standard companion/alternative to GPTQ for 4-bit deployment.

items.title: muP / muTransfer (Tensor Programs V)
items.innovation: Width-invariant HPs — tune small, transfer to large
items.summary: Scales init variances and learning rates so training dynamics stay stable as width grows, letting you tune on a small proxy and transfer to billion-param targets. Extensions: U-muP (ICLR 2025) and muP-for-MoE.

items.title: Compute-Optimal QAT
items.innovation: Scaling laws for when to switch to quantization-aware training
items.summary: Studies how to allocate compute between full-precision pretraining and the QAT phase, deriving when/how long to apply QAT for best low-bit accuracy. Practical recipes so QAT yields strong quantized models without wasting compute.

items.title: DeepSeek-V3 Technical Report
items.innovation: MLA + DeepSeekMoE + loss-free balancing + MTP + FP8
items.summary: 671B-total/37B-active MoE trained on 14.8T tokens for ~2.788M H800 GPU-hours, reaching GPT-4-class quality at a fraction of typical compute. The reference recipe for efficient frontier-scale MoE training.

items.title: Kimi K2 (MuonClip)
items.innovation: MuonClip (Muon + QK-Clip) — zero-loss-spike 1T-param MoE training
items.summary: A 1T-total / 32B-active open-weight MoE for agentic tasks, trained on 15.5T tokens with QK-Clip bounding attention logits as the headline stability technique. Top open-weight model on LMSYS Arena at release.

items.title: MiniMax-01 (Lightning Attention)
items.innovation: Production-scale linear attention hybridized with MoE
items.summary: 456B-total/45.9B-active MoE (32 experts) trained at 1M-token context, extrapolating to 4M at inference. Matches GPT-4o / Claude-3.5-Sonnet while offering a 20–32x longer context window.

items.title: Gemma 3
items.innovation: Higher local-to-global attention ratio to cut KV-cache cost
items.summary: Lightweight open multimodal models (1B–27B) adding vision, 128K+ context, and wider language coverage via distillation. 27B-IT approaches Gemini-1.5-Pro; 4B-IT rivals prior Gemma2-27B-IT.

items.title: Llama 4 Herd (Scout / Maverick)
items.innovation: Native-multimodal MoE; iRoPE for a 10M-token context
items.summary: Scout ~17B-active/109B-total (16 experts, 10M context); Maverick ~17B-active/400B-total (128 experts) surpasses GPT-4o on several benchmarks. Both distilled from the 2T 'Behemoth' (blog only, no arXiv report).

items.title: OLMo 2
items.innovation: Fully open (weights+data+code) with training-stability fixes
items.summary: Dense 7B/13B/32B models on the performance-vs-compute Pareto frontier, matching/beating Llama 3.1, Qwen 2.5, Gemma 2 with full transparency and the late-stage Dolmino data curriculum.
